channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 96 replies — 172.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-06-08 14:57 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62323880
>>62323245
>>62323584
>>62323600
YOU LOST, TRANNY

It doesn't matter if Bitcoin falls to 59k, I repeat what I said: it is good riddance that the yuppies go find another con to shove down people's throats because they are overleveraged and undereducated. No one wants their store value reserve being speculated on by retards who laugh as they sell the bottom, then complain they can't make money.

Stupidity is a tax on success.

Also, keep in mind, BTC might be 6% loss in a day, SILVER and GOLD are doing WORSE. You can't compare BTC performance against stock because it is not apples to apples, BTC can only be compared with other crypto in its asset class, and other storage value investment like PMs. And guess what? It still blows all of them out of the water, and the trade volume of BTC evaluated in any desk is far greater than all of crypto and rock collections combined. BTC brockerage platforms exist by the thousands, how many dealers of PMs exist?

I will put this even simpler for bear trolls: read the Bible, flip it open to the parable of the talents, and read what is said about the one faggot who thought he was smug and clever because he neither won, nor lost the money loaned to him. This is you, this is the whole bear market.

I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes. I'm going laughing my way to the bank enjoying this fire sale of BTC owned by the eternal underclass of statist peasants.

BTC will be 70k by the end of the month. Hell, it will be 60k+ by the end of the day.

If I called the bottom 10% above, it is because I overestimated the intelligence of the morons that do me a favor by forgetting this asset class exists.

May they buy ETFs and go hold the bag of billionaire scammers.

I repeat:
>if you bought, hold
>if you haven't, enjoy the sale
>if you sold, we are better without you

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

62326531
The falling knife is around $32.8k

Screencap this and thank me later.

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

item.title: If BTC had gone below 50k I'd have been able to buy a whole coin but nooo it had to pump up from 59k again. — 12 replies — 27.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
If BTC had gone below 50k I'd have been able to buy a whole coin but nooo it had to pump up from 59k again.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-08 15:04 UTC

OP

If BTC had gone below 50k I'd have been able to buy a whole coin but nooo it had to pump up from 59k again.

Early replies (first 3)

62335047
>>62335043
Jew at 5 seconds

62335086
Its insane how much effort westoid women have to exert just to look appealing past 25

62335150
>>62335086
I know a woman that looks really good at 29, but she used sunscreen and tretinoin with discipline. it's possible, and it's moreso with homebodies. that's partly why I believe people here especially on /pol/ say they look younger than they are because none of you fucking leave your houses.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335155
>>62335150
Its also why Aussie girls look older than they are. The sun is relentless

62335161
>>62335150
>none of you fucking leave your houses.
Good. Its where my guns anime food and bed are.

62335253
>>62335043
thats AI

62335421
>>62335150
>tretinoin
Are you zoomers fucking mad? Enjoy your fucked up livers. It's like Covid, immediate outcomes are dwarved by what happens the next decades. If you get a severe heat stroke or burn, your kidneys are mostly fine, and then they fail at 50% life risk decades later.

62335451
>>62335043
does she have head lice? why does she keep doing that hair thing?

62336068
>>62335043
Not-Asian/10

62336171
>>62335043
Whew lad. Looks like a 35-year old wine aunt attempting to recreate a 20-year old's video

62336174
>>62335086
>westoid

Are you implying chinks and jeets look BETTER as they age? LMAO

62336264
>>62335043
Man...I missed out on life.

item.title: What do you think outperforms over the next few years? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do you think outperforms over the next few years?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-08 15:15 UTC

OP

What do you think outperforms over the next few years?

A) All in TQQQ (3x Nasdaq-100 Index)

B) All in BTC

C) All in tech stocks/etfs (NVDA, MU, DRAM, VGT etc)

Early replies (first 3)

62336111
>>62336086
NERVGEN (ticket Ngen)

62336181
>>62336086
The halving isn't until 2018

62336247
SOXS

Buy SOLANA in november and hold it until 2029.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336265
Tech earnings are priced in 2 years out. Its probably gonna be energy/utilities, and if indexes crab cc funds.

62336269
>>62336111
Literally got trips you must all buy now

62336305
>>62336086
SGOV until 2018 and then probably TQQQ

item.title: 6 DOLLARS — 24 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
6 DOLLARS

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-08 14:14 UTC

OP

KEK BAGGIES

Early replies (first 3)

62327248
>>62327243
It just gets worse and worse for them.

crypto a shit.

62327252
I love the slow realization of all these bagholders that their staking and holding is just going to pay the salaries of chainlink labs as they sell off the token lol, they're so shameless about it.

there is no incentive for it to go up, retards. the fair value is probably sub $5 to operate oracles, it has no reason to go up.

62327413
Let's HECKIN GO
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329419
>>62327243
it's like a gift from Sergey at these prices

62331703
>>62327243
I trust Sergey.
I trust the white paper.
I trust the plan.
Never selling.
1k eoy.
SN=SN.

62331710
SERIOUSLY, I DON'T FUCKING GET IT.

LOOK AT FUCKING ZEC. IT HAS PUMPED $120 AFTER BEING EXPOSED. IT IS SO SHIT THAT THERE MIGHT BE AN UNLIMITED SUPPLY. AND NOBODY CARES. IT CRASHED BUT IT'S ALREADY PUMPING.

FUCKING WHY

LINK IS THE MOST FUNDAMENTAL CRYPTO IN THE ENTIRE SPACE. NO DEFI WITHOUT LINK


FUCK EVERYTHING JESUSCHRIST GOD

62331781
>>62331710
Basically TPTB aren't going to let us make it no matter what.

62331824
>>62331781
They do, but it's an IQ test, looks like you didn't pass muster

62331846
>>62331824
They don't, the stories of people making it are lies that are told to keep people motivated and on the treadmill.

62331855
>>62331781
But why can't we pump? It's madness. Even coins that have been exposed don't even crash that hard and then they keep pumping. It's pure madness. There might be an unlimited supply of ZEC that could have been used to make some insiders billionnaires. And it not only crashes to the ground, but after a crash that lasted hours, it begins to pump. ZEC is pure crap. Without LINK, defi wouldn't even exist. It's disgusting what is happening.

62331859
>>62331855
Everything is fake and gay.
EVERYTHING

62331864
>>62331855
honestly what you've said makes the chainlink thing so much worse. you're right, zioncash even after the literal confession of printed out of nowhere coins -> it STILL does a pump after.

Chainlink just continues dying for 6+ years.

62331873
>>62331846
if that helps you sleep at night, kek

62332520
how much link should I buy to make it

62332558
>>62332520
10k link tokens to be the future top 1%,
like having several billion USD now.
but you can get rich by holding 20-100 tokens, even one full token will be impressive in the future

62332565
Holding link is a fucking nightmare, holy shit.

62332823
>>62327243
I want $3 chainlink. I’ll buy 80k links at this price and then retire when chainlink goes $50 again

62332849
>>62331710
What value does Chainlink offer to society?

62332960
>>62332520
100k is make it stack

ignore all the faggots that tell you 1k or 10k link is enough

none of them made it with link in almost a decade. at this point ,

1/10 of a fucking century

62333015
>>62327243
>$7
wtf is this shit thread
you got my hopes up

62333505
Reminder that I will only start slurping when it hits $.15 and not a single cent more. You’re welcome.

62334743
>>62332849
Memes

62335034
>>62332849
lolcows

62336099
>>62331710
The trading bots are still picking up 2020 self-FUD in their algos. We buried our own graves linksissies (formerly marines) :(

item.title: >1-3% of your net worth in 30-60% out of the money puts at ALL times >This can be in any highly liquid volatile product… — 5 replies — 17.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>1-3% of your net worth in 30-60% out of the money puts at ALL times >This can be in any highly liquid volatile product…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-08 15:13 UTC

OP

>1-3% of your net worth in 30-60% out of the money puts at ALL times
>This can be in any highly liquid volatile product (SPX, NDX, BTC)
I just did the math. A crash always comes at the right time so you'll make back all the money you spent on the puts. And you are totally protected against crashes. You then put 7-9% of your net worth in fotm stuff for upside (SpaceX calls).
How do you even lose with this strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62335972
>>62335956
As the crash becomes more likely, the cost of the protective puts goes up. Just like with insurance and casinos, the house always wins.

62336037
Your risky bets go to zero and you match the market minus cost of puts

62336241
That is hedging. Try to backtest it. You can use markers like SMA200 to signal when you buy those puts.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336276
Ok but how far out though? Weeklies, 6 months out, 1 year?

62336295
>>62335972
The idea is to sell the puts outside the money. So that 97.5% of the time your puts don't get exercised. But, when they do, you got a good stock cheap.

item.title: Crypto used to be fun... — 24 replies — 16.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto used to be fun...

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-08 14:03 UTC

OP

Even in 2022-2023, there was still some life left in it. New narratives were still popping up and people were hopeful. The memes were still good. The mini alt run in 2024 was nice but even I knew that it was basically over given how much of a flop coinbase's onchain summer even was. It just continued progressively getting worse with all the scams that were going on and how pvp it became. The base chain itself was rigged with deep insider trading. Even if BTC breaks a new ATH, alts will never see the gains they used to. I honestly don't see the space ever going back to what it used to be, not even close.

Early replies (first 3)

62331836
AI took all the capital that normally flows into crypto. Guess the doggy coin meme mania killed crypto for good so no investors took it seriously. Also the web 3.0 meme was the perfect mid-wit trap to capture baggies.

62331860
>>62331829
the thesis behind crypto is non existent, there is no real world demand for a cryptocoin that will prevent it from going to $0

62331901
>>62331829
Only your second bear market, Anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62331942
>>62331829
Wait till OP learns about cycles

62331980
>>62331942
the 2025 cycle was completley cycled because of the ongoing stock market bull fueled by AI stocks. Crypto will come back eventually, but it's not worth it to wait around and cross your fingers, could be a long time.

62332020
>>62331836
Any other narratives left, or developing narratives that will help start up a new bull run?

62332034
The dumbest people on the planet were getting alts, and shitcoin in particular, this cycle. And there's literally nobody left to act as exit liquidity.

But a new sucker is born every day, so it'll recover eventually.

62332059
>>62332020
On the tech side: tokenized real world assets (the next big deal IMO), decentralized trading, prediction markets, DePin, privacy coins, agentic AI commerce (kind of a meme so far)… Plenty of narratives that lurk in the dark and can fuel another bull run.

On the investor side: millenials (the main investors in crypto) will inherit at least 50 TRILLION dollars from their boomer parents in the next 20 years. Overall the boomers, who are notoriously averse to crypto, will discharge more than 100 trillion dollars in the next decades. And the entire crypto market in only worth 2 trillion dollars so far.

62332067
>>62332059
is* only worth

Also, institutional investors coming in.

62332078
>>62331829
on short term, the pumps are still very massive and liquid, there's even more than in 2017.
on long term, the difference is that the trend for all alts seems to be to zero, but that doesn't prevent gains.

the only difference is now you have to be actively trading, the easy mode of just buying and waiting and get a x10 might be over.

it might come back if dollar dies and volatility increases.

62333458
>>62331829
I’m still bag holding projects from 2023 that are still trying to build off of old narratives. The team, and what’s left of the ‘community’ get excited over updates that mean nothing. It’s sad to see. The only reason why I still hold any of it is because they dumped 95%+ and they aren’t worth the gas fees to sell

62333534
>>62332059
>defi, ai, depin, rwa, privacy
So all of the same shit we have been hearing about for years now?

>millenials
The ones who made money are already out (or still barely in), and the ones who haven’t made it are still hanging onto their $100 bags worth of pepe, shib, and doge. Neither of these groups are going to do shit for the market. The newer generation don't give a single fuck about crypto either. Other than a few selective tokens out there, the space in general is cooked for real.

62333558
>>62331829
I get high from bullmarkets. It's my dope. I buy just to experience the euphoria from a wild bullmarket. I sell and wait for it to crash and do it again. It's fun. I'm still chasing the dragon of that first high but it keeps me hard. I love bear markets, every loser dips out and gives me cheapies. Bobo is your friend. Have fun anon. I have given up so many vices and pleasures so I can invest more. Drugs are a waste of money give me a good bullrun and I'm set. That's a wave I'll surf anyday. I'm so glad I wasn't so retarded to miss the semi boom.

62333601
>>62332078
> the easy mode of just buying and waiting and get a x10 might be over.
True. I don’t think that’s ever going to return, at least on a large scale like it used to be. Without being an insider or being part of some cabal, being a tradoor is too much if a risk. I doubt the average person has enough capital in the game for whatever potential gains to make any meaningful difference. It just doesn’t seem worth it. The market is too manipulated

62333606
I really doubt the next bull will be as heavily BTC focused as this last one where even the damn president of the united states held a speech during a Bitcoin conference with everyone clapping, cheering and hollering in the audience.

Sure the widespread completely crazy alt season like before will probably never come back, but the BTC dominance won't just forever go up either. All the neverending tokenization will spill over to at least some alts for sure

62333615
>>62333558
I wish I could feel that high again. Crypto made me apathetic. I feel nothing. It fucked me up

62333617
>>62331829
top kek mana is down 99% its like 0.06 now

62333638
>>62333615
>Crypto made me apathetic. I feel nothing. It fucked me up
thanks for the over emotional misery porn sentiment

a few more months of this crybaby talk in the catalog and I go all-in

62333716
>>62331829
Good. Link is the only “altcoin” that should even exist

62334344
>>62333638
That’s mean

62334386
>>62333534
There’s an enormous difference between a bunch of nerds *hearing* about things that are barely available like RWA or decentralized trading, and the irl financial world that actually goes all in on these things and does it at scale. Exact same difference between Anons holding BTC in 2013 and BlackRock having a huge BTC ETF in 2026.

Completely wrong on millennials. If they’re "already out", then literally who is in?

Basically you just let the bear market shape your view.

62334591
>>62331829
>Dotcoms used to be fun. Even in 1997-1998, there was still some life left in it. New narratives were still popping up and people were hopeful...

62335920
>>62334386
Millennials don’t have any influence on the market. What are you talking about? The ones who are driving the market are richfags that are trying to out trade each other with their bots and the market manipulators who are farming whatever liquidity is left

62336052
>>62335920
Crypto is almost entirely retail, and this particular kind of retail is dominated by millennials (and younger, non-boomer generations in general).

item.title: Crazy to think ETH is only $500 above the 2018 high what went wrong /b/ros? — 4 replies — 14.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-08 15:13 UTC

OP

Crazy to think ETH is only $500 above the 2018 high

what went wrong /b/ros?

Early replies (first 3)

62336208
Scaled too hard, token no longer needed

62336226
>>62336189
Patience is key. I remember after 2018 when ETH was trading between $150 and $300 for ages then it exploded and minted new millionaires. Same shit will happen again when all of a sudden ETH is trading at $10k+.

62336292
>>62336208
Staking? Defi? Trigger maxis?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336296
>>62336226
Esp according to my experience all of the weak handed niggers selling into the hand of lee and another inst, and me.....

item.title: AI Thread — 13 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI Thread

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-08 13:57 UTC

OP

AI is the technology that anyone can use to gain profitable revenue streams in their life from zero to little cost
But
What exactly should you do with it?
I am making this thread to discuss AI and learn about side business, hustles, hobbies, whatever you wanna call them that anyone can make to make money using AI
Strike while the iron is hot, pump as much as possible while we are bullish, etc
Tell me what the do with this shit NOW

Early replies (first 3)

62333163
>>62333151
It's a bubble. Personally I'd avoid.

62333660
>>62333163
is it though

62334852
>>62333163
people like this goy cant conceive of actually using the tool for money making things like algo coding or making clickbait ig boobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335329
>>62334852
What are the best uses
Is there anything you can do AFK or slight engagement only
Even just some slow weekend side thing would be fine

62335736
It can generate all kind of situations and learn from them. For example in FULL SELF DRIVING vehicles. So you could do other stuff while driving. If it's really worth the energy and water used for the chips I sure don't know

62335752
>>62335736
I'm sorry. You think it learns from itself without humans?

62335778
>>62333151
Well you can ask it to vibe code apps on par with something coded by 12 different amatuer coders that didn't talk to each other or review any existing code or standards as it progressed... So janky insecure apps basically

62335794
>>62335752
It can. Imagine Mario Kart racing bots against each other for one year. But it can simluate like a million years of all races with all kind of random situations. It will drive better than any human

62335799
>>62335794
Oh sorry. Can you show me exactly which model currently has RSI?

62335835
>>62335799
Not really. Any lab that actually achieved meaningful RSI would have enormous incentive to either keep it quiet or move extremely fast before announcing it. The public-facing research is always behind what's actually running internally

62335840
>>62335835
Anything public facing to do with any of this is simply a sales pitch.

62335955
Okay so how to make money with this shit retards

62336022
>>62335794
AI will do wonders for video games that's for sure

item.title: DAILY LAUGH AT STINKY LINKIES — 18 replies — 7.6/hr

OP

>Stink price june 2026: $7
ITS BEEN 6 FUCKING YEARS
6 WHOLE YEARS

Early replies (first 3)

62329620
god dayum son, now do it adjusted for inflation!!! OH NIGGER

62329632
>>62329612

>They'll say we got lucky!
More like you got too cocky.
kekbaggies.

62329640
>>62329612
>>62329632
Why didn't you sell in 2021?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329646
I never bought one red cent of your shitter because I refused to pump your bags purely out of principle.

62329663
>>62329612
six agonizing years of cuckoldry

62329677
>>62329612
BUT MY LINKIES AHHHHHHHHHHH

62331223
>>62329612
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62331265
>>62329612
Good for me since I was late to the party. Oldfag /litfit/ here, just bought for fun 400 LINK with part of my savings. Wish me luck, see you in 3-5 years /biz/. (also wish for all of you the best)

62331425
>>62331223
this cuckold is angry lol

62331431
>>62331223
look at this nigger seethe lmao

62331453
>>62329620
Lmaooooooooooooooooooo so the real price is like 3 dollars and still I am not incentivised to buy that shite...

Imagine the IQ of people who bought this shit lol

62331983
THE

62332001
>>62329612
And don’t forget a 2026 dollar is worth like 75% of a 2020 dollar

62332010
>>62329612
why should it go up in your opinion ? it's a token used to pay node operators
it wasn't made as a speculative asset

62332584
>>62331983
KEKOLDS

62332784
fuck all stinks

62333127
>>62329612
It puts the staking cage back on... No gains for you little cuckolds

62335770
asd

item.title: Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy? — 16 replies — 6.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-08 12:51 UTC

OP

What does BTC actually power? If Bitcoin were to go to zero tomorrow, it seems like very little in the broader economy would be directly affected.
Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications, including DeFi protocols and stablecoins. If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive, potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network.
Since a significant portion of DeFi and roughly $170B worth of stablecoins operate on Ethereum and Ethereum-related infrastructure, a severe and sustained decline in ETH could have much broader consequences for the crypto ecosystem than a similar decline in BTC. Am I missing something in this reasoning?

Early replies (first 3)

62333235
Maybe only 10% of anyone that knows anything about cryptocurrencies understand what Ethereum is.

62333256
>>62333235
Even after all these years?

62333335
No one cares about defi. Cyrpto has only one use and it is to provide liquidity that is proof against censorship, embargo, and currency debasement.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333354
>>62333256
For most people crypto is the same as Bitcoin. They might know about defi, stablecoins, NFTs and memecoins but for them it's tiny enough to be irrelevant. Their marketcap combined is less than BTC marketcap so this simplification is understandable.

62333441
>>62333196
It's a mystery to me too, but I can't ignore the charts and the ETH/BTC ratio, so I just hold BTC these days

62333512
>>62333196
>Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications
that are totally irrelevant
>including DeFi protocols
that nobody cares about
>and stablecoins
lol, lmao, even
>If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive
and nobody would notice
>potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network
"potentially" is doing a lot of heavy lifting there, and still nobody cares.
>If Ethereum were to go to zero tomorrow very little in the broader economy would be directly or indirectly affected
ftfy.

62333527
>>62333196
because this crypto economy is irrelevant compared to what bitcoin does

62333552
None of the shit built on eth pays eth, they charge a million fees then hash it and give eth 1 fee

62333710
>>62333527
>compared to what bitcoin does
yes, tell us what that is.

62333758
>>62333256
They clearly haven't read the website's homepage or the white paper because they call it Ethereum instead of Ether.

62333798
>>62333512
>and stablecoins
>lol, lmao, even
??

62334360
>>62333196
Anon, stop triggering the girls!

62334450
>>62333798
His reply has zero argument in it.

62335629
>>62333512
this guy gets it, it's incomprehensible slop thats had a decade to articulate itself and noone knows or cares

62335653
>>62333552
To add to this, base made like 95m last year and gave eth 5m. Imo there is probably a laffer-curve type goldilocks zone for fees. Idk how they would implement but they could probably triple the take and its still too low

62335710
Its proof of work instead of peice of shit
